stemHtml: Although hazardous ice crystals do NOT appear on an aircraft's weather radar display, pilots can usually avoid most of these areas in flight by...

options.a: looking ahead and spotting the crystal concentrations visually.
options.b: using the weather radar to identify and avoid CBs and the areas downwind of them.
options.c: requesting ATC to vector them around the areas of ice crystal concentration that appear on ground-based radar.
options.d: consulting the WAFC Significant Weather Chart, which have specific markings for forecast areas of ice crystals.
